entry.title: Re: i have to change abap/shared_objects_size_MB to a bigger value
entry.content:
Hi Maximo,

 As mentioned the steps are correct, select the instance profile not on default profile. before change the parameters please read the SAP note 2085980 and based on your SAP and kernel release.

Regards

SS

entry.title: SAP Mentor Interview with Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Earlier this month I had the opportunity to interview Eric van Rossum, SAP’s Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP. You can watch the video of our discussion and read the highlights below, where you’ll also find related learning materials. Enjoy!

Eric van Rossum is the Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP at SAP, overseeing product marketing for the company's cloud ERP portfolio. He serves as the strategic bridge between product development and customer acquisition, ensuring product messaging and positioning are attuned to customer needs, and facilitating the successful launch and adoption of products in the market. Eric has been with SAP for 24 years and brings a wealth of experience across various functions including consulting, sales, corporate strategy, and support.

To help me summarize the content of the interview, I've used SAP Generative AI Hub :-). Check here what it ays are the highlights of our conversation: 

  • SAP has two cloud ERP offerings: RISE with SAP and GROW with SAP
  • RISE with SAP is targeted toward SAP’s installed-base customers, providing them a path to cloud ERP at their pace.
  • GROW with SAP is targeted toward net new customers to SAP, and installed base customers who want to take a greenfield approach to cloud ERP.
  • SAP is focusing on making ERP an enabler for business model changes, driving sustainability, and improving efficiency, as these are the key customer needs currently in the market.
  • With RISE and GROW, customers should be able to update their software regularly, making them innovation-ready and efficient, especially with the increasing prevalence of AI.
  • Eric believes the shift to the cloud also requires a paradigm shift within companies, citing an example of a customer who moved towards a standardized system and adopted SAP's out-of-the-box solutions.
  • One of the significant hurdles for customers adopting cloud ERP is the organizational readiness to standardize their processes, which requires a significant cultural shift.
  • Moving ERP to the cloud is seen as risky by some companies due to the critical role ERP systems play in company operations. They need strategies to transition without disrupting their business.
  • Eric is interested in a continued dialogue with the SAP Community, to hear from end users and improve SAP’s offerings to meet their needs better.

If you’re going to Sapphire in Orlando or Barcelona, you can hear more from Eric in these sessions:

SAP Sapphire Orlando (June 3-5)

  • Unlock Business Agility with Cloud ERP (Session code: ERP169)

  • Supercharge Your Business and Get Innovation Ready with RISE with SAP (Session code: ERP168)

  • The New ERP Mindset (Session code: TRE321)

SAP Sapphire Barcelona (June 11-13)

  • Unlock Business Agility with Cloud ERP (Session code: ERP146)

If you’re planning to join Sapphire virtual, you can hear more in these sessions:

  • ERP126 | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition: Discover planned innovations

  • ERP127 | SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Find out what is next

  • ERP200v | Accelerate business growth with S/4AHANA Cloud Public Edition

And to learn more about the topics discussed in this interview, the following learning journeys are especially recommended:

  • Exploring SAP Cloud ERP learning journey as a starting point to start learning about SAP Cloud ERP, how to distinguish SAP’s cloud ERP solutions and transformation offerings (GROW & RISE), establish a cloud mindset (fit-to-standard, SAP best practices, integration and extensibility, clean core), and get insights into business AI and sustainability fundamentals
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition learning journey teaching fundamental implementation knowledge for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition leading to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition implementation consultant certification complemented by quarterly live sessions, stay certified content and assessments focusing on AI capabilities and intelligent ERP innovations
  • Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition learning journey teaching implementation fundamentals for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition leading to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition implementation consultant certification, complemented by stay certified content and assessments focusing on how to Manage a Clean Core

Those fundamentals learning journeys are complemented by various line of business specific learning offerings and certifications focusing on business processes, best practices content, and configuration/integration/extensibility options

entry.title: SAP Collaborative ERP adds Share as Card option to SAP S/4HANA Cloud – Microsoft Teams integration
entry.content:
As AI continues to take over more routine functions for knowledge workers, the remaining tasks often fall to experts or teams of experts. So, collaboration becomes even more important so people can complete tasks in real time, rather than delaying critical decisions because of latency in communication. This SAP S/4HANA integration with Microsoft Teams adds another option to SAP’s collaborative ERP, allowing people to share content as a card, in addition to the share as tab or share as chat functions.

With SAP S/4HANA 2402, we added the ability to share apps as a card, based on the collaborative stage view in Microsoft Teams. This allows the recipient to directly edit the data in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition within the Collaborative Stage View of Microsoft Teams. The value is similar to our other share with Microsoft Teams options – allow people to get more done without task shifting.

One example of how this might work is if one colleague asks another to remove a delivery block on an order. This new capability allows them to collaborate via chat in Microsoft Teams while simultaneously interacting with the relevant data in SAP S/4HANA.

Collaborate using the Collaborative Stage View of Microsoft Teams.

 

To summarize the collaborative ERP capabilities in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition that are generally available, we allow Microsoft Teams users to

  • Share as Chat – include a link to the SAP S/4HANA app in a Microsoft Teams chat
  • Share as Tab – display the SAP S/4HANA app in a tab in Microsoft Teams
  • Share as Card – use the collaborative stage view in Microsoft Teams to show the SAP S/4HANA app and allow users to interact with it

We are planning to add to the Share as Card functionality by providing the ability to share apps as an Adaptive Card-based Loop component. This will allow users to directly view and take action on the data in the Adaptive Card, which can be shared via Microsoft Teams or Microsoft Outlook. If you are an SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customer and want to test this functionality, please sign up for our early adopter care program.

entry.title: Re: Korean language in SAP MII

Regards

entry.title: Re: Excel formula to SQL formula for query
entry.content:
If you are looking for a basic SQL query to translate, try this : 

Select D * CEILING((B - C) / D ) AS result FROM your_table;

entry.title: Re: Z-tcode creation for CJ8G
entry.content: Why do you need a Z t-code? Have you tried creating a batch job with steps? You could have 50 steps each calling one of the 50 selection variants which equate to each company code.

entry.title: CJI3 submit program issue
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

Im facing a issue while submit the CJI3 transaction in my custom program.

some projects getting ALV data but some projects not found in CJI3 t.code getting this Pop Up message.

I need to suppress this Popup message if projects not found in CJI3 report.

 cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>set(
    EXPORTING display = abap_false
    metadata = abap_false
    data = abap_true ).

    DATA : it_tabs TYPE TABLE OF abaplist.
    DATA(prof_db) = '000000000001'.
    SET PARAMETER ID 'CAC' FIELD kokrs."'BCCA'.
    SET PARAMETER ID 'PDB' FIELD prof_db."'000000000001'.
 
    SUBMIT rkpep003 WITH SELECTION-TABLE it_rsparams AND RETURN.

    TRY.
        cl_salv_bs_runtime_info=>get_data_ref(
        IMPORTING
        r_data_descr = data_desc ).
      CATCH cx_salv_bs_sc_runtime_info.
    ENDTRY.

What should i do for skip this message in program.

entry.title: Re: Routing with Parameters in SAPUI5 application
entry.content:
@IanYin  The viewLevel is especially relevant for flip and slide transitions. It helps the router to determine the direction of the transition from one page to another. 

Setting the viewLevel to 2 helps the router to determine how to animate the slide transition. Navigation from viewLevel 1 to viewLevel 2 will be animated with a “Slide to Left”” animation. In contrast to that, the back navigation from the viewLevel 2 target to the viewLevel 1 page will be animated with a “Slide to Right” animation, due to the lower viewLevel than the target.

 


@minjie_lao  I hope you're not implementing an app that exposes the parameters values as described in this blog. Exposing values in the URL is the worst security practice. I don't even know why this was still considered and advertised in 2021.

entry.title: Unlocking AI Potential with SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP)
entry.content:
As SAP Sapphire 2024 approaches, we invite you to explore the exciting world of AI within SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). Whether you are attending in person at Orlando or Barcelona, or joining us virtually, these resources will empower you to infuse AI into your applications and drive innovation

BTP Interactive learning tools: Get guidance with available SAP resources covering aspects like application development, integration, data analytics, and AI

  1. Build Code Test Drive: Experiment without risks 
  1. Code Samples: Generative AI hub starter kit 
  1. Reference architecture: Optimal setup guides 
  1. Step-by-Step Guidance: Detailed implementation instructions on SAP Discovery Center 

Knowledge Building Tools: Collaborate and learn how to use Business AI to implement use cases with certification programs and hands-on developer tutorials

  1. Tutorials: Prompt LLMs in the generative AI hub in SAP AI Core & Launchpad | SAP Tutorials 
  1. Certification Programs: Recognized learning paths for Business AI 
  1. Developer Videos: Hands-on videos on BTP use cases with AI 
  1. Community: Be part of our new public group for AI enthusiasts 

Expert Assistance: Find resources to support your BTP AI journey

  1. SAP Certified Partners: Find SAP Partners 
  1. Tailored SAP Support: SAP Support Portal

entry.title: Re: SAP Mentor Interview with Chief Marketing Officer for Cloud ERP

entry.title: Re: Z-tcode creation for CJ8G
entry.content: Hi Ken , Actually there is no specific interval to run CJ8G . Business want to run in foreground whenever needed . Supposing with 5 company code , we will create one selection variant . Now we want to assign one Z-tcode to this variant . Whenever user run this Z-tcode , variant should be defaulted . This is the requirement .

entry.title: Re: Can you remove Onboarding business process error mess...
entry.content:
Hi @sshah1 ,

Currently as standard practice there is no such manual option to delete the errors in BPE screen rather only we have restart option.

But if you go BPE GUI and delete the process definition of those errors manually this will remove in the BPE error screen however The Engineering team has advised NOT to delete Process definitions from the BPE: Manage Process definition page. 

Refer this KBA Notes: 
3134782 - [Onboarding] Issue with deleting process definition from BPE GUI

Hope this helps!!

Thanks,

kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Re: Adding candidate to Talent Pool from Requisition
entry.content:
Hi @Roxanne1 ,

Hope you are doing good!!

Please find KBA notes: 2710896 - How to Add a Candidate to a Talent Pool Directly From the Candidate Workbench - Recruiting Management

For more information refer to the Candidate Relationship Management Talent Pools.

Hope this helps!!

Thanks ,

Kr,

karthick.

entry.title: Requirements Oracle user for data services repository
entry.content:
Hi,

We are currently in the works of creating local / central repositories. I have a few questions about the database user requirements on an Oracle database.

In the installation guide i find the following to setup a database account for a local repository. 

"Ensure that the database accounts have privileges to create, modify, and delete tables, and to create stored procedures."

My question is what privileges does the database account need when the repository is running? (After installation). Are these the same privileges?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Requirements Oracle user for data services repository
entry.content: Please refer Extra requirements for Oracle documentation for SAP Data Services to use Oracle for the repository database.

entry.title: Re: May Developer Challenge - AI Services - Week 5
entry.content:
Hello, 

Here is my submission for this week.

entry.title: OCI Config Update
entry.content:
We are using OCI catalogues and we have to maintain procurement outline agreement in below configuration in HTML Field and HTML Value for NEW_ITEM-CONTRACT and NEW_ITEM-CONTRACT_ITEM. 

SPRO > SAP Ref. IMG – Plant Maintenance and Customer Service - Maintenance and Service Processing - Maintenance and Service Orders - Interface for Procurement Using Catalogs (OCI) - Convert HTML Field Values

Could you please suggest any way or standard application to maintain this directly without transport request or client open. 
This is very frequent activity in case of master data change and very tedious to do by transports. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: OCI Config Update
entry.content:
These are considered customizing settings and for that reason they would be transported into production from a lower system. 

Perhaps you could create a Conversion Module to set these fields. You can implement custom logic to determine the field values. If you have fixed values that change regularly then consider using a ztable. For further information see the help associated with the customizing point 'Interface for Procurement Using Catalogs (OCI) > Define Conversion Modules'

A further possibility would be to develop a utility program to directly set these fields in table IOCI_FCONV.

entry.title: Re: Certification exam on SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi Shyamala,

Interestingly, "Datasphere" was not mentioned in the recently updated SAP Certification Recommendation. There are many retired and new certifications, but there is no current or upcoming certification specifically for SAP Datasphere.

I think the new SAP Data Engineer - Data Fabric Certification that will be introduced in June will also cover SAP Datasphere.

Regards,
Tuncay

entry.title: Re: Send mail service not working
entry.content:
@naveenvishal 

The OData service is expecting a jwt token to be passed. Try generating a jwt token using the credentials of the xsuaa service and setting to the Authorization header in the below format.

Authorization : Bearer <token>

entry.title: Spanning of SAP Companion content from one Fiori App screen to connected Fiori App screen
entry.content:
When we open a particular Fiori App, we launch the In-app help panel and create a Guided Tour, as displayed below in picture 01.

Fiori App Name: Manage Suppliers

Guided Tour Name: Create New Partner Function

Next, on the Manage Suppliers Fiori app, when we start creating new Supplier (Partner) organization by clicking New >> Organization, the connected Fiori screen appears as shown below. This new screen does not show the Guided Tour we created on the previous Fiori screen.

My Question: Does a Guided Tour created on one Fiori app screen continue on the connected Fiori screen as well? Does the spanning of SAP Companion Guided Tour from Fiori screen to another connected Fiori app screen possible now? I know that earlier it was not possible.

entry.title: SAP Companion Help appears on the Fiori App Home screen, but not on the related Fiori screen
entry.content:
Using SAP Companion, we are authoring In-App Help (Guided Tours) content for SAP Fiori apps. In case of some Fiori apps, we see the In-App Help icon on the Fiori App Home page/screen, but when we navigate to the related Fiori screens, the In-App Help icon does not appear.

For example, below is the homepage of the My Change Requests Fiori app screen. On this screen, I can see the In-App Help icon, and I added a Guided Tour as “Test A”.

Now, on the My Change Requests Fiori app, when I open a particular change request, the related Fiori screen appears as shown below. However, on this screen, the In-App Help icon is not there.

My question – Any reason why in some cases, the In-App Help icon/panel appears on the Fiori app home screen, but not on the related Fiori screens?

entry.title: Activate suspended provider contracts in SAP BRIM which has been expired
entry.content:
Hi All,

There is a scenario in which we get a suspension and we suspend the provider contract. This is a migrated provider contract with no preceding document. However, while the contract is suspended, the provider contract expires. Now the thing is the client doesn't want to create new orders or contracts and asking to activate the same contract which has been expired during suspension. How can we handle this scenario ?

entry.title: What’s new with SAP Build Work Zone guided experiences
entry.content:
Navigating complex business processes can be daunting, often leading to inefficiencies, mistakes, and user frustration. To address these challenges, both SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, and SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone offer a differentiated capability called guided experiences. This feature enables business users to simplify complex business processes into self-service journeys that are easy to follow and execute. In this blog, we will explore what a guided experience is, how it works, the latest enhancements, and a couple of real-life customer examples.

An overview of guided experiences

Guided experiences are designed to simplify multi-step business processes by breaking them down into manageable and intuitive steps. Each step can be enriched with various types of content, including videos, documents, forms, tasks, UI cards, and apps to provide users with all the necessary information to complete each step. Steps can be classified as optional or mandatory, ensuring critical tasks are completed while allowing flexibility for other actions. Users don't need to worry about finishing all the steps at once as the progress is saved within Work Zone, enabling them to resume from where they left off. 

Guided experiences can be used for various complex processes such as parental leave, benefits enrollment, employee relocation, new hires onboarding or suppliers onboarding, training and enablement, and browsing and purchasing electronic equipment.

New hires onboarding guided experience

Supplier enablement guided experience

Create, modify, and extend guided experiences with low-code

Creating a guided experience in SAP Build Work Zone is a straightforward process. It can be done through low-code and without IT dependency! The building process is streamlined through a wizard-based layout, supported by different types of content, such as videos, documents, UI cards, and apps, which can also be found on other workpages. This enables the reuse of pre-built and custom content as well as SAP-delivered content, boosting efficiency. The wizard layout can be added to any workpage within a workspace or in the overall menu. The low-code approach also allows business experts to quickly build and deploy guided experiences without requiring extensive technical know-how. 

Set up a guided experience through a wizard

Looking ahead in Q3, business experts can create a multi-step process in SAP Build Process Automation. They can then use it as a process template called guided process to create a guided experience in Work Zone, which includes pre-built content from SAP SuccessFactors. This eliminates the need to recreate the process from scratch and accelerates the entire process from building to rolling out the experience for production.

Real-life customer examples using guided experiences

A customer has integrated SAP Build Work Zone with SAP Concur to simplify business travel planning. When employees need to plan travels to a production facility or an overseas office, they are guided through a series of questions to ensure necessary preparations are made, such as the need for an office workstation or proper approval. This streamlines the travel request process. This customer is also looking to use guided experiences as an intake form to collect information and flow this information into SAP Build Process Automation, then to SAP Task Center, triggering notifications and task updates for business users. 

Another customer uses guided experiences to develop comprehensive learning journeys on different subjects, such as IT and leadership, tailored to the specific needs of different audiences. These learning journeys incorporate both structured and unstructured content from various sources, such as SAP SuccessFactors Learning and LinkedIn Learning. This approach ensures that employees receive targeted training in a well-structured and easily accessible way, thus improving their overall learning experience.

Value delivered through guided experiences

The guided experiences in SAP Build Work Zone are changing the way businesses manage complex processes. By breaking down these processes into easy-to-understand visual elements, business experts can rapidly build, modify, implement, and deploy the experience to improve efficiency, accuracy, and user satisfaction. This also helps improve end users' overall experience, allowing them to accomplish tasks more efficiently.

Learn more

Visit SAP Build Work Zone, start your free product trial, and engage in the Community to learn more.

entry.title: Re: SAP Companion Help appears on the Fiori App Home scre...
entry.content:
Hi Angad,

Is the second screen really Fiori or another technology like GUI for HTML or Web Dynpro?  If it is Fiori, modify the URL of the application by adding help-editor=true following the ? in the existing URL.  It should look something like:

This should force SAP Companion to be displayed.  If it doesn't, please confirm that it really is a Fiori app and if it is, raise a ticket using to Component ID KM-SEN-CMP to get someone to check the issue.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Downloading data into multiple work sheets in excel
entry.content: it s work good logic

entry.title: Re: Draft Report with Bin Locations - Goods Receipt PO
entry.content:
Hello!

You don't necessarily have to integrate the tables into a single query.  You can use a technique called UNION ALL.

 

SELECT 'Invoice' AS "Type",T0."DocNum", T0."DocDate", T0."CardCode", T0."CardName", T0."DocTotal" FROM OINV T0
UNION ALL
SELECT 'Credit' AS "Type",T0."DocNum", T0."DocDate", T0."CardCode", T0."CardName", T0."DocTotal" * -1 AS "DocTotal" FROM ORIN T0

 

You can see that I have effectively two independent queries (but you can run the entire thing in a query window as a single query).  One from OINV and one from ORIN and I have even manipulated the DocTotal so that in the credit side of the query it is negative (scroll right).  As long as all of the columns are the same datatype and there are the same number of them then you can join the queries this way.

You can then extend this and sort them by turning the whole mess into a derived table:

 

SELECT X."Type", X."DocNum", X."DocDate", X."CardCode", X."CardName", X."DocTotal" 

FROM
(
SELECT 'Invoice' AS "Type",T0."DocNum", T0."DocDate", T0."CardCode", T0."CardName", T0."DocTotal" FROM OINV T0
UNION ALL
SELECT 'Credit' AS "Type",T0."DocNum", T0."DocDate", T0."CardCode", T0."CardName", T0."DocTotal" * -1 AS "DocTotal" FROM ORIN T0
) X

ORDER BY X."DocDate"

 

So I just put parenthesis around the UNION ALL query and labelled it as "X" and then outside of the parenthesis you can refer to the columns with the X alias.  In my example I wanted it to sort by "DocDate".

This is identical in SQL Server / HANA.

With your example you can just do a query for the GRPOs and then stack it on a query for the drafts you want.  With BIN locations and stuff it gets too complex to unify it the way you are trying to do it.

Let me know if this makes sense,

Mike
Check out my YouTube SAP Business One channel: http://youtube.battleshipcobra.com/

entry.title: Re: Analytical list page not working after changing filte...
entry.content:
I am also facing the same issue.

In my scenario, I am using SAP Fiori Elements Analytical Page using CDS with Parameters.

On loading the application and on providing selection filter for the first time, the results are populated both in chart and table properly.

But when I change the selection filter and retrigger the GO action, both the chart and table are frozen with no data being displayed.

Not sure if we can use Analytical page with CDS and parameters?

I dont see much help on SAP blogs for the same. Can anyone help?

entry.title: Logistics Simplified: Resources, Insights, Workshops, and More for Smooth Adoption
entry.content:
Introducing you to the SAP Digital Logistics Adoption Campaigns!

Our mission is to empower you with the knowledge and tools needed to fully leverage the capabilities of Logistics solutions. This program is designed to help you understand the unique value these solutions bring to your business, guiding you through every step of the adoption journey. You’ll find comprehensive resources, expert insights, customer experience sharing, and practical tips to accelerate implementation, ensuring a smooth Go-Live, and maximizing the benefits for your business!

Our current campaign focus is on S/4HANA Transportation Management and S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management. Join us on this journey through just a few simple steps!

Step 1: Access the Solution Value Card

A value card is a one-pager with key information on the solution, such as its value proposition, key capabilities, and quick recourses. It also indicates what to expect from the Adoption campaign. 

  • S/4HANA Transportation Management
  • S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management

Step 2: Participate in Expert Guided Adoption Series

To further support your journey, we have organized workshops that are crafted to provide in-depth training, featuring interactive sessions, real-world scenarios, and expert-led discussions. Whether you're just getting started or looking to deepen your expertise, these sessions will equip you with actionable strategies and best practices to drive successful adoption and consumption. Join us to enhance your skills, connect with peers, and challenge us with your unique questions so we can understand your needs better!

S/4HANA Transportation Management

  • Getting Started with S/4HANA Transportation Management: PDF | Recording
  • June 25, 2024: Baseline Activation and Setups
  • July 18, 2024: Optimizer & Package Building
  • August 20, 2024: Insights & Analytics

S/4HANA Extended Warehouse Management

  • Overview & Value Realization: PDF | Recording
  • Feature Implementation Best Practices: PDF | Recording
  • June 13, 2024: Industry Best Practices: Consumer Products
  • June 27, 2024: Industry Best Practices: Automotive
  • July 11, 2024: Industry Best Practices: Retail
  • August 8, 2024: Business AI Adoption in Logistics

We hope these sessions enhance your understanding and ability to leverage the solution, and we hope to see you in one of our upcoming workshops! If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to email us.

entry.title: Navigating Finance and Quote-to-Cash Solutions: Resources, Insights & Workshops for Smooth Adoption
entry.content:
Introducing you to the SAP S/4HANA Group Reporting Adoption Campaign! 

Our mission is to empower you with the knowledge and tools needed to fully leverage the capabilities of SAP Finance and Quote-to-Cash Solutions. This program is designed to help you understand the unique value these solutions bring to your business, guiding you through every step of the adoption journey. You’ll find comprehensive resources, expert insights, customer experience sharing, and practical tips to accelerate implementation, ensuring a smooth Go-Live, and maximizing the benefits for your business!

Our current campaign focus is on S/4HANA Group Reporting with assets for more products coming soon. Join us on this journey through just a few simple steps!

Step 1: Access the S/4HANA Group Reporting Value Card

A value card is a one-pager with key information on the solution, such as its value proposition, key capabilities, and quick recourses. It also indicates what to expect from the Adoption campaign. 

Step 2: Check out the Expert Guided Adoption Series

To further support your journey, we have organized workshops that are crafted to provide in-depth training, featuring interactive sessions, real-world scenarios, and expert-led discussions. Whether you're just getting started or looking to deepen your expertise, these sessions will equip you with actionable strategies and best practices to drive successful adoption and consumption. Join us to enhance your skills, connect with peers, and challenge us with your unique questions so we can understand your needs better!

  • S/4HANA Group Reporting: An Introduction (Public and Private Cloud): PDF | Recording
  • S/4HANA Group Reporting: A Closer Look (Public and Private Cloud): PDF | Recording
  • How to Adopt Group Reporting in Public Cloud – Accounting （Public Cloud): PDF | Recording

We hope these sessions enhance your understanding and ability to leverage the solution! If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to email us.

entry.title: Re: Item category determination in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi varunvenkat,

Thank you for this blog, very insightful.  May i know if SAP has a documentation that lists standard item categories and how/where it's intended, meaning what's the related business process for such item category?  You used the example "CBLN represents a free of charge item, which means a product with that item category won’t be billed later on", which is good... but how about the rest?  I can see there are hundreds of these categories.  I'm new to SAP S/4HANA Cloud, is there a documentation for each category that will guide me in starting the learning and understanding the functionality of each of these categories?

Regards,

Cathy

entry.title: Req to create 2 sales order using bapi with the data from JSON payload by using SAP odata GW

Hi All,

I need to create multiple sales orders using a BAPI in SAP using sap gateway call.I am getting data in deep entity format(JSON FILE).Kindly suggest me which method i can enhance so that i read the payload data in DPC ext class

entry.title: Re: migrate data from SAP R/3 (4.7) to SAP S/4HANA Cloud...
entry.content:
Hi @KaviM,

There is no direct data transfer from SAP R/3 to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. For the direct migration approach, the lowest supported SAP ERP release is ECC 6.0 (SAP NetWeaver 7.0).

I would recommend exploring the Staging Table approach.

Hope this helps.

Arturo

entry.title: Modernize your Commerce Stack with Composable Content Management
entry.content: Ever wonder how you can build more meaningful and seamless digital commerce experiences? It’s simple, you need reusable content blocks that are flexible, scalable, and incredible. That’s why SAP Commerce Cloud and Contentful have partnered to deliver a solution that helps businesses deliver top of the line customer experiences. Join us to learn how the partnership between SAP Commerce Cloud and Contentful has helped organizations create meaningful customer experiences from comprehensive business insights to individualized shopping experiences. Register for the webinar here.

SAP Guru's, For PR REL Strategy with classification, it is prompting info error about the long text. 

So, in our SAP system, we're already using a class. Now I created a new class for my region but it is not letting me add Rel Group - Rel. Obj - Class (new). Why?

Rel. group Rel. obj. Overall rel.    Class      Description

011_  FRG_EBAN     Group 01
021_  FRG_EBAN     Group 02
S11X  FRG_GF_EBANGroup S1
S21X  FRG_GF_EBANGroup S2

entry.title: Re: How to rename cell Totals - SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
- hide the total of the "Tonnes" column - value 335.000.

Click on the cell with value 335.000 and from styling menu , change the font to White color

entry.title: IBP to Datasphere with mass extraction of key figure data
entry.content:
Hello Folks!

I'm having problems doing mass extractions from IBP to Datasphere. First i've tried the OData APIs, but According to the API documentation: "If you need to extract large amounts of data, we recommend that you do so using SAP Cloud Integration for data services."

 

After digging for a while, however, I've discovered that the service is no longer available on BTP:

What Is the best practice in this case, then? Extract the data using CI-DS? Or should I just go to Integration Suite with Cloud Integration Like in this blog ?

entry.title: Re: Restrict to close Production Order If Issue & Receipt...

entry.title: Re: Restrict to close Production Order If Issue & Receipt...

entry.title: How to make an Outbound delivery not relevant for POD?
entry.content:
Hi,

Currently, in our sales process, the delivery is relevant for POD. I understand this is because the delivery item category and BP both are marked for "POD Relevant".
Could anyone please help how can I make the delivery not relevant for POD? If I mark the delivery item category and BP not relevant for POD, then during Outbound delivery creation the system throws the error - "Delivery item 000010 of delivery is not relevant for POD".

Request to please provide your inputs to make the process not relevant for POD.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Login to SAP Digital Assistant service at BTP using SAPDAS cli
entry.content: Joule is not specific to success factors. As of now, it is available with success factors. The availability of Joule as a service is planned for 2024 and will be made available soon.

"Application object BUPA: Table BUS000FLDS does not exist"

Request to please help in fixing this issue.

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Customer with multiple distribution channels
entry.content: In short, a customer who is assigned both the distribution channels .i.e. '03' AND '10' should NOT be displayed in the report while customer who is assigned distribution channels - '02', '07' , '10' should be displayed. In either cases, distribution channel - '10' is common

entry.title: Re: SAP DM - Order POD only show work when available at w...
entry.content:
Please try the below if it works for you. 

Go to POD Designer to create a copy of order pod. you have selection to default your WC, material etc. Also, you can enable show sfc status filter so that user can select the sfc status and then result can be filtered accordingly. 

This way, a much suited result set can be obtained. Alternatively, you can plan to extend via PPD, UI etc and have your own UI screen to your needs if that fits your requirement better.

entry.title: Re: How to write Data into S4 Odata via CAP
entry.content:
Hello Rewati,

The Database transactions are automatically managed within an OData servicer event, and we don't have to open a new transaction! The CAP community has maintained excellent documentation, you can refer to these:

1. The CRUD actions can be done through the CQL, similar to SQL statements.

2. Adding external service to a CAP project.

3. Transaction Management.

 

I hope this helps!

Harsha

entry.title: Re: Wily Introscope offline no matter what :-(
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

The 404 error in the defaultTrace is a known issue which has been recorded in KBA 2352112 - Enterprise Manager is offline in Infrastructure Preparation due to error 'com.sap.smd.introscope.em.IntroscopeException: http status code 404' - SAP Solution Manager 7.1 and 7.2. 

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: FSCM Credit Magt: Manual score update
entry.content:
In Standard SAP you cannot overwrite the score. The reason being that the score is used to derive the Risk Class and the Risk Class can already be defined manually.

However, you could overwrite the score using a workaround. You can for example, define a feld under "Creditwothiness data" and use it to enter the desired score manually.

Then create a new scoring rule that calculates the score as equal to the value you have already entered in the new field manually. 

So you would still need to execute "calculate score" under Credit profile for the BP, but the score will be equal to your manually entered value.

entry.title: Failed to load destination. Caused by: No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly neces
entry.content:
Hi, I am attempting to call an SAP OData service from a CAP (Cloud Application Programming) application using a CDS spawn job. I am utilizing a destination with Principal Propagation but encountering the following error:

logs:-
[cds] - connect to sap_odata > odata-v2 { destination: 'sap_destination', forwardAuthToken: true }
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR [remote] - Error: Error during request to remote service:
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR Failed to load destination.
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at run (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/utils/client.js:310:31)
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at async RemoteService.<anonymous> (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/_runtime/remote/Service.js:276:20)
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at async next (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:76:17)
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at async RemoteService.handle (/home/vcap/app/node_modules/@sap/cds/lib/srv/srv-dispatch.js:74:10)
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at async Object.methodName(/home/vcap/app/srv/util/myfile.js:102:24)
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR at async mycatfile (/home/vcap/app/srv/util/mycatfile.js:524:22) {
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR statusCode: 502,
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR reason: {
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR message: 'Error during request to remote service: \n' +
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR "Failed to load destination. Caused by: No user token (JWT) has been provided. This is strictly necessary for 'PrincipalPropagation'.",
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR request: {
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR method: 'POST',
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR url: '/xxxx/xxxxxx_SRV/dummyEntitySet',
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR headers: {
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR accept: 'application/json,text/plain',
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR 'accept-language': 'en',
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR 'content-type': 'application/json',
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR 'content-length': 311,
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR authorization: 'Bearer ...'
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR }
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR },
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR correlationId: '507a3d4f-bf6d-4296-4cc1-f6b8f070ee2b'
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR }
2024-05-29T21:07:57.42+0530 [APP/PROC/WEB/1] ERR } 

code :- 

 

const myFn = async (req, data) {
    cds.spawn({ 
          user: cds.context?.user,
          tenant: cds.context?.tenant,
          headers: cds.context?.headers || req?.headers,
          after: iafterTime }, async (req) => {
          sendToSAP(req, customID)
    })
    return 'processing in backgorund'
}

const sendToSAP = async (req, customID) => {   
    const payload = {}  
    const oSAPService = await cds.connect.to("sap_destination")
    let headers = { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
    headers.authorization = cds.context?.headers || req?.headers
      const response = await oSAPService.send({
        method: "POST",
        path: "/xxxx/xxxxxx_SRV/dummyEntitySet",
        data: payload,
        headers: headers
      });
}

 

 

 
"dependencies": {
"@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client": "^3.0.1",
"@sap/cds": "6.8.4",
"@sap/cds-dk": "6.8.3",
},
"engines": {
"node": "^18.0.0"
}
 
this is workign without spawn job.

entry.title: Data Action to create values for existing dimension members but yet no values
entry.content:
Hi all,

i am trying to create a Data action which creates a specific value (zero, not Null) for a given time horizont and for the members of each dimension I selected beforehand via prompt.

First approach:

MEMBERSET[d/Dim1]= %Paramater1%

MEMBERSET[d/Dim2]= %Paramater2%

etc...

Data([d/Measures] ="Measure1")=0

also etc...

This DA only writes back zeroes for already existing values.

My desire is to "fill in" the null values with zeroes in this case.

Any idea? TYIA

entry.title: Vector Engines Overview
entry.content:
Well, this is not a question but my general understanding of Vector engine and my personal note of it.

Vector Engine

SAP previously used SAP HANA cloud as the database which was a multi-model database. Now with the release of vector engine (in sap Hana cloud) we can store the data in form of vector embeddings also.

Now also the database is multi-model. We have just added the vector engine to the database.

 

So, SAP already had these types of models like spatial data, graph type data, JSON etc. Now we have a vector embedding type of data also which is managed by the vector engine.

SAP does not only store these data or embeddings but also perform analytical computations with them to leverage fast query and provide contextual data to LLM’s.

 

We have a new data type to store these types of data namely -> REAL_VECTOR.

A constructor -> TO_REAL_VECTOR.

And two new distance calculating functions -> l2distance(), cosine_similarity().

L2Distance() calculates the Euclidean distance between the two vectors.

Cosine_similarity() is based on the angle the two vectors make with each other, More angle means more similarity.

These distance calculations are important as they help to calculate the relation between two vectors and how closely they match with each other.

 

We can directly query the vectors using normal SQL-like syntax from our application layer. Not just query but also perform in memory computations and also use the vectors for Retrieval Augmented Generation(RAG) to get the contextual data from the LLS’s used.

 

What is vector embeddings?

These are Mathematical data representations of any objects in a multidimensional vector space. These vector embeddings are generated by AI which are good with certain data types like maybe pdf or word.

It converts the unstructured data to a format that can be processed quickly.

Now that we have vector embeddings, these can be used to find the matching record using the distance in the vector space. All the related docs are kept at one place so the distance is less.

 

 

 

From this figure we can understand the whole use case.

We have uploaded some pdf files to the cloud database. The DB will create the chunks of texts and convert it into vector embeddings using AI and store it in vector space in the vector engine in HANA DB. Now this data is accessible throughout the DB.

A user comes and queries for the word “DATABASE”, this query is now converted to a vector itself and with the use of semantic search it queries the DB with the vector of our string. We might not have the exact word in our DB. Now this query is processed through the LLM model for giving the answer, as we don’t have database string in our DB. It will look for the closest thing possible logically based on the distance measurement technique. Therefore, fish is not considered but the string ‘TABLE’ is given as output.

Hence we have utilized the vector engine in order to send contextual data regarding our own DB data to the LLM to give accurate results with least hallucinations. This is the concept of Retrieval augmented generation also(RAG) which uses third-party data source apart from the dataset used to train the LLM.

entry.title: Re: Connecting to Cloud Foundry provided by BTP trial
entry.content:
Hello Felix,

before login to CF, please check that platform user you are using to connect to Cloud Foundry is both a member of an org in Cloud Foundry and a space developer member in a CF space.

Please also try to use the SSO Passcode option where you have to open an URL with your platform user and then enter a provided sign-in passcode for login in BAS. 

Best regards,
Peter

entry.title: Re: dynamically resize width of tables next to each other?
entry.content: thank you @JefB . I have voted for your impr. request.

entry.title: Re: Upgrade SAP Business Application Studio to SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Hi Dan,

SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) is our offering for professional developers and lets you easily create extensions.

SAP Build is a suite of products containing SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation, SAP Build Work Zone, and SAP Build Code. 

If you are interested in the value proposition of SAP Build Code, here they are:

  1. It is a holistic offering comprising design-time and run-time capabilities for cloud development – you get all the services needed for cloud development together.
  2. It has a simplified commercial model.
  3. It has Joule support in the design time, allowing you to leverage generative AI for building your applications.
  4. It automatically ensures that you follow the BTP Developer’s Guide and always follow SAP best practices. In the IDE, many guides help you to create and deploy your first cloud applications (Full-Stack, UI)
  5. It lets you manage your projects via the SAP Build Lobby enabling fusion development with the other SAP Build products.

Hope this helps.

entry.title: First SAP Inside Track Event in Auckland
entry.content:
Join the Auckland SAP Inside Track for an insightful discussion on the latest trends and best practices in SAP technology. This event is designed for professionals who are eager to deepen their knowledge of SAP Business Technology Platform.
Network with like-minded individuals, share experiences, and gain valuable insights that can enhance your skills and career opportunities. Whether you are an experienced SAP professional or just starting in the field, this meetup promises to provide valuable takeaways and foster connections within the Auckland SAP BTP community.

Come hear from a couple SAP Champions as well as some local BTP #rockstars!

Agenda:
1. SAPPHIRE updates - BTP Growth and Acceleration!
2. SAP BTP Architecture - Best Lessons Learned
3. SAP Cloud Application Programming (CAP) - Riding the Learning Curve!
4. How to help your organisation be a Future Ready Enterprise (FRE)

RSVP now to secure your spot at this informative event!

There will be drinks and nibbles after the event.
Space is limited so register early!

Register using the below link
https://www.meetup.com/auckland-sap-btp-meetup-group/events/301356488/

Tuesday, June 25, 2024
4:00 PM to 5:30 PM NZST
Location:  Lagom, 16 Anzac Avenue · Auckland

entry.title: how to stop migration cockpit long time running job
entry.content:
Transaction Code: SE16N

Table name: /LTB/JOB_QUEUE

 

1. find project UUID in migration cockpit project

Project UUID: LVkQorLa7jwjlTnEwu8IaG

 

2. find the OBJECT UUID in the migration cockpit project

OBJECT UUID LVkQorLa7jwjlU0}vLdItG

3. find the user ID who blocks the job

4. input above collected value into Table name: /LTB/JOB_QUEUE

5. get the activity UUID and activity

Find the activity UUID - 993645B344D61EDEB48F939CD8CE6FB3

6. clear or delete the blocked activity.
